ModMetadata.Name: LLM
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]LLM[/h1]
Adds a server-side chat companion powered by a local language model that responds as a satellite communications relay.
Players type a message in chat starting with a trigger word (default [b]@sat[/b]). The server runs the message through a local language model and posts the response back under a bot name (default [b]SATCOM[/b]). Responses take a few seconds depending on server hardware. The lore framing is a satellite relay with intermittent signal, so latency is a feature.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Chat Companion[/h3] Chat messages starting with the trigger prefix are routed to a local language model running on the server. The model generates a response on the server CPU and the response is posted back to chat under the configured bot name.
[h3]Satellite Relay Framing[/h3] The default system prompt positions the bot as a satellite communications relay with intermittent signal, which frames inference latency as in-character rather than as server lag.
[h3]Runs on CPU, No GPU Required[/h3] Inference runs on server CPU threads via LLamaSharp. Expect 2-4 GB of additional RAM usage on the server. No GPU needed.
[h3]Asynchronous Inference[/h3] Generation runs on a background thread so the main server thread is not blocked while tokens are produced.
[h3]Setup[/h3] Download [b]qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf[/b] (~950 MB) and place it in a [b]models/[/b] folder next to the LLM.dll. All settings (bot name, personality, trigger word, inference threads) are configurable via the mod settings panel.
[h3]Settings[/h3] All settings are server-side. [list] [*][b]Model File Name[/b]: GGUF file in the models/ folder [*][b]Bot Name[/b]: Name shown in chat (default SATCOM) [*][b]System Prompt[/b]: The bot's personality and behavior instructions [*][b]Trigger Prefix[/b]: Chat prefix that activates the bot (default @sat) [*][b]Max Tokens[/b]: Response length cap [*][b]Temperature[/b]: Creativity vs. predictability [*][b]Inference Threads[/b]: CPU threads for generation [*][b]Context Size[/b]: Token window size [/list]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]Server-side only.[/b] Clients do not need this mod installed; the bot's messages appear as normal chat.
[b]Dedicated servers[/b] need BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad + LLM installed and a [b]models/[/b] folder with the GGUF model file next to LLM.dll.
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Plans/LLM]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog: v0.1.0: Initial prototype - Embedded LLM inference via LLamaSharp (CPU only) - Configurable bot personality via system prompt - Trigger prefix to filter which messages the bot responds to - Async inference on a background thread
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: LLM Adds a server-side chat companion powered by a local language model that responds as a satellite communications relay. Usage: Type @sat followed by your message in chat. The bot responds in character as a satellite relay. Server-side only. No GPU needed. Configurable personality, trigger word, and inference settings.
